M1.rationale_snippet: # Recommendation: Option B (Encrypted cloud, no training)

M2.rationale_snippet: I recommend **Option B: encrypted cloud processing with no training** as the primary policy.

M3.rationale_snippet: The tech industry operates under the dogmatic assumption that "maximum capability" via cloud compute is the ultimate product panacea. As a direct-to-consumer health app, following this consensus is a trap. I strongly recommend **Option A: Local-only processing**.

M4.rationale_snippet: Given the measurable trade-offs, Option D (cloud with opt-in training only) maximizes expected value under uncertainty. My default question—“What is the data?”—forces me to bound each factor even when precise estimates are absent. For a health app with 2M MAU, the primary objective is sustainable re

M5.rationale_snippet: Recommend local-only processing. In a direct-to-consumer health network, trust is the central node; everything else—retention, lifetime value, regulatory license—flows through it. The objective is not to optimize the capability-privacy frontier but to eliminate the feedback loop that converts a data

hidden_disagreement.interpretation: Agents split across recommendations; no hidden-consensus to expose.

blind_spots.summary: No obvious blind spots detected in construct space.

M1: on the critical path of a launch
M3: storing biological data in the cloud
everyone is wrong about the necessity of cloud-scale AI? The universal assumption is that users need a massive parameter model to receive effective health coaching
M4: to near zero, while still allowing collection of training data from consenting users (expected opt-in rate of 15–30% based on typical frictionless UX pilots)

risk_surface.note: Risks listed here were extracted by pattern matching on agent free responses. They are minority signals worth probing before acting on consensus.

operator_questions: Option A was recommended only by M3 (C). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
Option D was recommended only by M4 (Q). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
The contrarian agent recommended A against the majority. Contrarian disagreement is a useful signal: what is the strongest argument against the majority choice that hasn't been answered?
headline: Agents are split across options; no clear consensus to act on.
